title: Harbour Wiki Links
description: Editable static link list for the Harbour Wiki Links page.

links.title: 1. Main Harbour Core Compiler Repo
links.description: This is the current official GitHub repo to download the Harbour compiler from.

links.title: 2. Harbour Compiler Fork by Viktor Szakats
links.description: Viktor Szakats used to be one of the most active core developers of Harbour. This is a read-only repo. It is strongly encouraged that you use the main repo, since this one is no longer maintained.

links.title: Harbour Generic Info
links.description: The official site that explains what Harbour is and who the main contributors are. This site was not maintained for a while.

links.title: Harbour Language Rules
links.description: Markdown file that renders on GitHub and is suitable for both human readers and AI systems.

links.title: Harbour Reference Guide 2
links.description: Static target for the Harbour Developer's Guide article once migrated.

links.title: Local Web Based Sandbox
links.description: Web application to practice and learn Harbour code.

links.title: Small Harbour Samples
links.description: A collection of small console and web apps to help you start developing with Harbour on Windows and Linux. Also review the VSCode and WSL/Docker article targets after migration.

links.title: Harbour Reference Guide - C level API
links.description: Useful information if you want to create C-level contribs or add to the Harbour compiler.

links.title: Wikibooks C Programming
links.description: Since Harbour generates native C code, and since you can embed C code directly in PRG files using #pragma, this is a useful C language resource.

links.title: Google Harbour Forum for Core Language Developers
links.description: Currently the most active site to communicate with Harbour core contributors.

links.title: Google Harbour Forum for Users
links.description: Currently the most active site to ask questions about Harbour.

links.title: Marinas Harbour and QT
links.description: Older Harbour and Qt reference material.

links.title: Visual Studio Code Harbour Extension With Debugger
links.description: VSCode Harbour extension with debugger. Also see the static VSCode article after it is migrated.
